question: Which quarter shows the best overall restaurant performance based on the stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q3 shows the best performance because it has the highest combined bar height.
options.B: Q1 shows the best performance because it has the lowest kitchen temperature and fewest customer complaints.
options.C: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it stacks different metrics that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.D: Q1 shows the best performance because all three indicators are at similar levels.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
